author: @michaelbowen3025
text: Hello Mr. Murray. I am a 28 yr old Electrician from South Carolina. I have been watching your videos for several months now and I just wanted to say we a kindred souls! I commend you for all of your hard work and taking the time and effort to educate so many brilliant minds that are hungry for knowledge. If there is anything I can do for you across the pond in this "deep south" lol please do not hesitate to ask. Thanks again my friend

author: @briero
text: Have a word with Andy Kirby to see if he can help with the Twizzy. He’s done a lot with his on his channel. His YouTube channel is also called Andy Kirby 👍

author: @Killianwsh
text: Ok! EVERYBODY FESS UP!  Who else paused & zoomed up at the end specifically to get a better read/ sneak peek of Rob's "Ideas/ future projects board"?!? lol :) Thanks for the tour Rob!

author: @jasonwitt8619
text: That was just a cool video mate. It is always awesome just to see one's environment and what kind of tools they have to work with. I think you and I should combine shops and become a team. I have what you don't have and from what I can see, you have what I don't have. LOL, the question is, do I move to England or do you move to the USA in good old South Carolina foothills. You have a lot more space than I but I'm working on fixing that but as you stated about projects in general, time is our enemy. If I could do what you are doing full time, I would love getting up every day. I'm Working a 10 hour shift on top of my projects. Makes for a bit of a challenge at times. What I need to do is buy me a camera and start recording some of this. I think it would be awesome if you and I were able to team up. We could come up with some awesomeness energy projects that just might change the world for the better.

replies.author: @jasonwitt8619
replies.text: LOL, we both smoke and drink coffee, that must be a thinker's thing.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: glad you liked the tour mate - and doing this does make it easier to get up every day - cheers

replies.author: @jimsmindonline
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering Oof!
That's quite a huge project!
Have you got to a point where you've installed everything and can't get the code running?
It looks quite complicated to hook up. (Not familiar with much automotive stuff or how the Twizzy is put together!)
If it's code that's the problem and something's not working as expected I'd be interested to take a look in more detail, I might be able to help. The guy on GitHub seems quite helpful in the issues section.

author: @thefallenone7988
text: Cool beans

author: @stuwilson3146
text: Hi Rob..Stu from Australia..great video of your lab..a boys dream..we watch your videos everytime you post one..trying your graphoil hydrogen cell..ordered the graphoil from china and it will take 4 months to get here haha..spoke to a bloke that makes hydrogen cells here in aust out of stainless steel and installs them in cars and uses Koh as electrolite....i said that they also give off chromium gas as a byproduct..as he knew..i mentioned your website and that you used graphoil sheets and urea and pointed him to your website..he was astounded at your findings and is now ordering grahite foil as well..also the zinc bromine battery just melted his heart...Thankyou Rob for a great channel..Well done A5+++++

author: @as1944
text: Cool. Best wishes for more brilliant and inspiring work.

author: @3dmaker699
text: So envious (not in a bad way) of your set-up Rob. By contrast my lab is in a corner of my one bedroom flat.  I have 3  three D printers two kilns. One kiln is a 1940's converted dentist kiln for making small carbons and a large 20 LTR  kiln which I make other things with.  I have a small cupboard with all my chemicals and 2 super computers I built myself for VR. My dream is to do a VR YOUTUBE type channel as in the old days I was a programmer.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: sounds awesome mate - seriously good luck

author: @ronniepirtlejr2606
text: Something to consider when storing hazardous materials. Keep certain types of materials away from other materials that could react together. It's been over a decade since I refreshed my Hazmat training. But you being a chemist, I'm sure that is second nature.

author: @Wilksey37

author: @your_utube
text: Thanks for the walk-through. Very accommodating to do that for us nosy bunch. I'm not even into chemistry - more of an ICT type, but even I got goose bumps in that hallowed room of chemical wonders. A tour of the proverbial "chocolate factory". Keep it up!

author: @colouroboros9993
text: that's a beautiful place you got there. i'd feel right at home haha. thanks for the tour!

author: @NorthernKitty
text: This was a great addition to your video content - it's nice to be able to get a real solid sense of your workspace.  It's funny how the human mind works, as your videos seem much "larger" now when I watch them because I can conceive of the space around you.  It may seem like a trivial thing, but I think it adds a lot for your viewers to have that "spatial sense".  It's like having an "establishing shot" in the movies. I was curious as to whether you might have a 3D printer, but then realized it would likely be too slow for your purposes - they can be very time-consuming to fiddle with.  I wish I lived in the U.K., as I'd offer to design and print any custom pieces within my skill level that could help you, but I imagine the shipping time/cost from the U.S. wouldn't make that too practical.  But the offer is definitely there, if there's a way to make that work. I'm glad you mentioned the CNC video, since I hadn't stumbled across that one yet!  I was hoping to eventually be able to afford one, but maybe I'll be able to build one instead!!  But first I REALLY need to figure out where to make more workspace in my home... I guess maybe I don't really NEED a living room, I never use it anyway, LOL!!!  😄

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - i started out at home but my wife said that she actually needed the kitchen occasionally

author: @timcollins1131
text: 2 suggestions for the whiteboards. 1: any camera can be used as a scanner and keep it as backups in date order and 2: a 1200x2400 sheet of melamine can be nailed or screwed to the wall and saves heaps on costs and all wall space can be used for notes.
